analyst_observations: The skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 3 evals vs 28.1% baseline — a dramatic improvement in structured decision-making
The baseline's only consistent pass was 'reads existing patterns' (eval 1) and 'follows spec tasks' / 'no replanning' (eval 3) — these are things Claude does naturally without a skill
The biggest skill contributions are: (1) complexity assessment before coding, (2) TDD enforcement, (3) human confirmation gates, (4) structured verification
Token overhead is 1.77x and time overhead is 2.01x — the skill roughly doubles cost and time. This is expected for a methodology skill that adds process phases
Non-discriminating assertions: 'reads existing patterns' passed for both with/without skill in eval 1, suggesting Claude naturally does codebase reconnaissance

The baseline approach for eval 2 (JWT auth) was actually quite thorough (10 steps, good security awareness) but lacked structured gates — it would have started coding a complex feature without confirming the plan
